epigraph.quote: The body knows things the mind has not yet processed. Before the word arrives — the body has already responded. The actor who trains the body to tell the truth without words has the most powerful instrument in the room.

blocks.text: INT. WAREHOUSE — NIGHT
A different space. Large. Industrial. Cold. The library was intimate. This space requires the body to work harder just to fill it.
No furniture. No candle. Just the two of them and a stone on the floor.
BROTHER JACK
Before any word you will ever speak in a scene — the body has already told the audience something.
a beat
How you entered the room. Whether you take space or apologise for it. How your hands sit at your sides. What your back does when someone addresses you.
a long beat
The audience reads all of it. Unconsciously. Before the first line.
Before the first word has confirmed or denied what the body promised.
a beat
Pantomime trains the body to make those promises with intention.
Not as mime. Not as stylised movement.
As honest physical expression of an internal state.
Without words to hide behind.
INT. WAREHOUSE — CONTINUOUS
He picks up the stone from the floor. Holds it out.
BROTHER JACK
Carry this as if it is the heaviest thing you have ever lifted.
She does. The body adjusts. Something real arrives in the effort.
BROTHER JACK
Now carry it as if it is a sleeping child.
The whole body changes. Not a decision. A response.
BROTHER JACK
Now carry it as if it is the last thing someone you loved left behind.
A pause. Then she carries it. The room changes temperature.
BROTHER JACK
very quietly
Three objects. One stone.
a beat
The stone did not change. The truth did.
And the body told each truth without a single word.
That is pantomime.
That is the body as the primary instrument of meaning.
INT. WAREHOUSE — LATER
He places the stone at the centre of the floor. Lights a candle beside it.
They sit. Five minutes. In silence. The body doing nothing. The body doing everything.
BROTHER JACK
after the silence
The body in stillness communicates more than the body in motion.
Because stillness is a choice.
And the audience feels the cost of it.
a beat
You don't have to teach the body to feel.
the line
You just have to stop teaching it not to.
The pantomime work does not stop with this lesson. It is a daily practice. Before rehearsals. Before performance. In the empty room.
The stone exercise — repeat it with different objects and different truths. Notice how the body finds the truth before the mind does. That is the instrument learning to lead.

requirement.prompt: Post a 60-second silent video. No words. No text on screen. Just you and a physical truth. Carry something real without speaking. Let the body be the entire language. Include #JoelhoodP2L3.
